description: Four-panel conceptual benchmark-construction and sealed-evaluation workflow.
notes: The final PDF and PNG are the visual ground truth; these are non-numeric source notes.

description: Herb-target and herb-compound records were obtained from Dryad version 6; Dryad documentation attributes the herb-related records to HIT 2.0.
notes: The Benchmark v1 herb-target source file was byte-identical to the Dryad-distributed file.

description: The source-record block lists 87,825 herb-target corrected positives, 14,985 herb-compound edges, 1,456 high-strict compound-target edges, and 2,536 target-target pairs.
notes: Values are displayed in the final figure.

description: Target identifier harmonization is followed by deduplication and endpoint screening; H0847 is excluded before the formal eligible-positive count of 86,317.
notes: Represents the benchmark asset construction workflow.

description: Exact normalized-name grouping defines leakage-control units before a group-aware train, validation, and sealed-test split.
The figure displays 535 / 67 / 67 herbs and 69,053 / 8,632 / 8,632 positives for train, validation, and sealed test.
notes: Values are displayed in the final figure.

description: Configuration selection uses validation, followed by final five-seed training, validation-AUPR checkpoint selection, and sealed-test access precheck.
notes: The protocol is frozen before sealed-test access; no post-test tuning or model reselection is allowed.

description: Each held-out herb is scored against all 2,309 target candidates using permitted label-free compound context.
notes: Evaluation labels are applied after scoring; unrecorded pairs are unlabeled candidates.
